title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
summary: Military historian Sarah Paine argues that global power dynamics for two millennia have been shaped by a divide between continental empires pursuing security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers pursuing prosperity through trade (Britain, US). The lecture examines how the Industrial Revolution altered this framework and explains contemporary geopolitical conflicts through this lens.
writeup: Military historian Sarah Paine argues that global power structures over two millennia have been shaped by a divide between continental empires that pursue security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers that pursue prosperity through trade (Britain, the US). She uses this geographic framework to interpret the behavior of Putin and Xi as constrained by the logic of land-based expansion rather than open commerce. The takeaway is a model of geopolitical competition in which geography conditions whether a state seeks conquest or trade.
